hero.title: Coffee Talk Tokyo Wiki
hero.description: Coffee Talk Tokyo is a cozy visual novel set in a late-night Tokyo cafe. Brew hot and iced drinks, meet humans and yokai, follow Tomodachill clues, and unlock character endings.
hero.playOnSteamCTA: Play Coffee Talk Tokyo on Steam

coffeeTalkTokyoReleaseDatePlatforms.title: Coffee Talk Tokyo Release Date and Platforms
coffeeTalkTokyoReleaseDatePlatforms.intro: Coffee Talk Tokyo is the newest late-night café entry in the Coffee Talk series, set in modern Tokyo with humans and yōkai sharing stories over carefully brewed drinks. The official publisher page and live store listings show a May 21, 2026 release, with standard editions, demo access on supported stores, and Deluxe upgrade content available on several platforms.

items.editions: Standard game, Deluxe Edition, Deluxe Edition Upgrade DLC

items.system: Nintendo Switch; supported on Nintendo Switch 2
items.demo: Check the Nintendo eShop page for regional demo availability
items.editions: Base game with Deluxe Edition Upgrade DLC

items.demo: Free demo available on PlayStation Store
items.editions: Standard Edition; Deluxe Edition with Seattle Prologue Chapter, 10 additional in-game City Pop tracks, and Digital Artbook; Deluxe Edition Upgrade add-on

items.system: Xbox One, Xbox Series X|S, PC, and handheld-compatible Xbox ecosystem devices
items.demo: Check the Xbox store page for regional demo availability
items.editions: Standard Edition; Deluxe Edition; Deluxe Edition Upgrade add-on

coffeeTalkTokyoBeginnerGuide.title: Coffee Talk Tokyo Beginner Guide
coffeeTalkTokyoBeginnerGuide.intro: You play as a barista in a Tokyo café where humans and yōkai visit for drinks, music, and heart-to-heart conversations. Most progress comes from listening closely, choosing the right temperature and ingredients, and using side tools like Brewpad and Tomodachill to understand recipes and customer stories.

steps.title: Read the customer’s request first
steps.action: Look for clear words such as hot, cold, coffee, tea, yuzu, honey, milk, mint, mango, lychee, ice cream, or whipped cream.
steps.why_it_matters: Orders are often written like natural dialogue, so the clue is usually hidden inside the customer’s mood or wording.

steps.title: Choose hot or cold before ingredients
steps.action: Pick the drink temperature first, then choose the base ingredient.
steps.why_it_matters: Coffee Talk Tokyo recipes start with temperature, and some story orders only work when served hot or cold.

steps.title: Build every drink in three ingredient slots
steps.action: Choose a base, then a primary ingredient, then a secondary ingredient.
steps.why_it_matters: The primary ingredient has more influence than the secondary ingredient, so the same ingredients in a different order can make a different result.

steps.title: Learn the main bases
steps.action: Use Coffee, Tea, Green Tea, Hojicha, Chocolate, and Soymilk as your core recipe families.
steps.why_it_matters: Most recipes are easier to solve once you first identify the correct base family.

steps.title: Use flavor clues to finish the recipe
steps.action: Add ingredients such as Yuzu, Ginger, Honey, Mint, Mango, Lychee, Whipped Cream, or Ice Cream when the customer mentions taste, fruit, spice, sweetness, foam, or a float.
steps.why_it_matters: Story orders often describe taste instead of naming the final drink.

steps.title: Add latte art or stencils when requested
steps.action: Use stencil and latte art tools when a customer asks for something cute, pretty, decorated, or visually special.
steps.why_it_matters: Coffee Talk Tokyo adds stencil latte art as a visible drink-customization feature.

steps.title: Check Brewpad and Tomodachill
steps.action: Use Brewpad to review special drinks you have discovered and Tomodachill to follow customer profiles, story hints, and social updates.
steps.why_it_matters: The game gives recipe and character clues outside the main dialogue, so checking side apps helps you avoid missed context.

steps.title: Fix mistakes before serving
steps.action: Reset an unbrewed drink, trash a wrong drink before serving, or reload a save if you want a cleaner route.
steps.why_it_matters: You can correct a bad brew before it becomes a story response.

coffeeTalkTokyoDrinkRecipes.title: Coffee Talk Tokyo Drink Recipes
coffeeTalkTokyoDrinkRecipes.intro: Coffee Talk Tokyo recipes begin with Hot or Cold, then a base ingredient, then a stronger primary ingredient, then a lighter secondary ingredient. The full game includes 54 special Brewpad recipes across Coffee, Tea, Green Tea/Matcha, Hojicha, Chocolate, and Soymilk families; the rows below focus on high-use story drinks and easy recipe anchors for the homepage.

items.story_use: A simple black coffee order and a reliable bitter coffee answer.
A sweet coffee choice that works well with latte art requests.
Useful when a customer asks for iced coffee with ice cream.
A strong Indonesian-style coffee with ginger.
A basic milk-tea anchor for early tea orders.
The direct answer for yuzu-and-honey tea orders.
Cold version for iced yuzu honey tea requests.
A fruit-heavy tea option for orders asking for plenty of fruit.
The clean answer for traditional matcha requests.
Cold matcha for iced matcha requests.
A named early route drink for Makoto.
A late-route drink for a forest-in-the-snow clue.
The plain roasted-tea answer for warm Hojicha requests.
A recurring safe answer for Yuki’s usual iced Hojicha.
A mojito-like tea drink for tangy and minty clues.
A late-story favorite tied to Yuki and Fuku.
A hot chocolate option for honey-and-milk-style requests.
A cold chocolate mint drink for double-mint clues.
A spicy cold chocolate answer with a richer finish.
A soymilk float choice for Erika’s lychee order.
A warm, fruity, sweet drink for comforting bedtime-style clues.
A warm soymilk, ginger, and honey drink for spicy-sweet orders.

coffeeTalkTokyoWalkthrough.title: Coffee Talk Tokyo Walkthrough
coffeeTalkTokyoWalkthrough.intro: Coffee Talk Tokyo runs through a sequence of café days where correct drinks improve customer outcomes and keep best-end progress on track. This homepage walkthrough preview focuses on drink results, important mechanics, and route milestones while keeping the full story details for the dedicated walkthrough page.

steps.route_focus: Tutorial, hot drinks, first stencil request
recommended_drinks: Bee’n Buzzy with latte art and a stencil for Ayame
steps.milestone: Introduces the basic brewing loop, the first drink, and latte-art/stencil achievements.

steps.route_focus: Cold drinks, toppings, and the first larger customer set
steps.milestone: Unlocks cold-drink routing and introduces ice cream or whipped-cream style orders.

steps.route_focus: Yuzu tea, Hojicha routes, and caffeine checks
recommended_drinks: Any cold drink for Jun’s quick order
Pink Skiff and Iced Hojicha for Erika
steps.milestone: Covers the early yuzu, hojicha, and caffeine clue pattern.

steps.route_focus: Mango, matcha, sweet-spice clues, and stronger improvisation
steps.milestone: Moves the route from simple named recipes into flavor-based clue solving.

steps.route_focus: Fruit-heavy orders, repeat favorites, and character-specific preferences
steps.milestone: Highlights repeated favorites and the importance of remembering earlier customer patterns.

steps.route_focus: Final route checks and best-ending setup
recommended_drinks: Mango Melba for Yuki and Fuku
steps.milestone: Finishes the main café route and prepares the end-of-story character outcome sequence.

coffeeTalkTokyoCharactersAndYokai.title: Coffee Talk Tokyo Characters and Yokai
coffeeTalkTokyoCharactersAndYokai.intro: Use this character guide as a quick entry point for the new customers, major route names, and story roles connected to Coffee Talk Tokyo's best endings and social posts.

items.name: Kenji
items.story_focus: Kenji is searching for meaning and a way forward after retirement.
items.route_note: His best-end achievement is Punching Out, Going Home.

items.name: Vin
items.story_focus: Vin is coping with a fractured past and the daily consequences of a previous disaster.
items.route_note: His best-end achievement is With A Little Help From My Friends.

items.name: Ayame
items.story_focus: Ayame is trying to understand her new afterlife while still keeping her bright, playful energy.
items.route_note: Her best-end achievement is Last Hurrah in Ghost Town.

items.name: Ash
items.story_focus: Ash is one of the named routes tied to the game's best-ending achievement set.
items.route_note: Ash's best-end achievement is A New Page.

items.name: Blue
items.story_focus: Blue is tied to route scenes involving Vin and the Blue Nights credits moment.
items.route_note: Blue's best-end achievement is Foot in the Door.

items.name: Jun
items.story_focus: Jun's route connects to inspiration, transformation, and a dragon-related achievement.
items.route_note: Jun's best-end achievement is Inspiration From the Source.

items.name: Makoto
items.story_focus: Makoto appears in the route set and is connected to change and self-renewal.
items.route_note: Makoto's best-end achievement is New Me Activated.

items.name: Emi
items.story_focus: Emi is part of the main customer group and has a route conclusion in the ending sequence.
items.route_note: Emi's best-end achievement is Out Of Office.

items.name: Erika
items.story_focus: Erika's route appears in the credits-based best-ending achievement set.
items.route_note: Erika's best-end achievement is Peer Review: Approved.

items.name: Fuku
items.story_focus: Fuku appears early in the story and has a dedicated best-end route.
items.route_note: Fuku's best-end achievement is Desk Jockey No More.

items.name: Yuki
items.story_focus: Yuki is one of the main character routes followed through to the ending snapshots.
items.route_note: Yuki's best-end achievement is Goodbye Hiromi.

items.name: Akiko
items.story_focus: Akiko is connected to Ayame's route and a post-credits violin scene.
items.route_note: Related achievements include Messenger Between Worlds and The world's warmest violin.

coffeeTalkTokyoEndingsAndChoicesGuide.title: Coffee Talk Tokyo Endings and Choices Guide
coffeeTalkTokyoEndingsAndChoicesGuide.intro: This section gives players a clean overview of how to aim for best endings without turning the homepage into a full walkthrough.

items.heading: How endings work
items.summary: Serve the right drinks, pay attention to dialogue, follow character updates, and let the credits roll after Day 15 to see the ending snapshots.
details: Dialogue choices influence character outcomes.
Correct drinks matter for the best-drink route and the Voted Barista of the Year achievement.
Tomodachill clues can point players toward hidden story context and better route results.
Best End achievements unlock during the credits sequence when the route conditions have been met.

items.heading: Drink order rules
items.summary: Coffee Talk Tokyo drink recipes depend on temperature, base, primary ingredient, secondary ingredient, and sometimes latte art or stencil use.
details: Ingredient order matters because the primary ingredient is stronger than the secondary ingredient.
Some best drinks are special recipes, while a few accepted orders are off-menu drinks.
Cold drinks, whipped cream, ice cream, latte art, and stencils are all tied to early achievement goals.

items.heading: Best End route list
items.summary: The main best-ending set covers Ash, Ayame, Blue, Emi, Erika, Fuku, Jun, Kenji, Makoto, Vin, and Yuki.
details: A New Page: unlock Ash's Best End.
Last Hurrah in Ghost Town: unlock Ayame's Best End.
Foot in the Door: unlock Blue's Best End.
Out Of Office: unlock Emi's Best End.
Peer Review: Approved: unlock Erika's Best End.
Desk Jockey No More: unlock Fuku's Best End.
Inspiration From the Source: unlock Jun's Best End.
Punching Out, Going Home: unlock Kenji's Best End.
New Me Activated: unlock Makoto's Best End.
With A Little Help From My Friends: unlock Vin's Best End.
Goodbye Hiromi: unlock Yuki's Best End.

items.heading: Full best-ending completion
items.summary: Finishing the game with all best ends leads into the full-route completion goal.
details: Seeing the Best in Yokai is the achievement for finishing the game with all best ends.
One Way to Find Out unlocks after playing story mode to conclusion once.
True Blue Nights, Messenger Between Worlds, and The world's warmest violin are route-related scenes that completionists should track.

items.heading: Spoiler-light first playthrough advice
items.summary: Check every customer request carefully, avoid rushing dialogue, and review Tomodachill before moving forward.
details: Use the Brewpad and customer wording together instead of guessing by drink name alone.
Open Tomodachill when new posts or hashtags appear.
Replay route days after finishing if a character snapshot or achievement is missing.

coffeeTalkTokyoTomodachillGuide.title: Coffee Talk Tokyo Tomodachill Guide
coffeeTalkTokyoTomodachillGuide.intro: This guide turns Tomodachill into a clear player checklist: check profiles, follow hashtags, read hidden posts, and connect the clues to route progress.

items.name: Character profiles
items.what_it_does: Tomodachill lets players learn more about customers outside the café conversation scenes.
items.player_tip: Open new or updated profiles after story shifts so you do not miss route context.

items.name: Clickable hashtags
items.what_it_does: Hashtags can lead to hidden posts from characters you do not currently follow.
items.player_tip: Tap related hashtags when they appear, especially before ending-route decisions.

items.name: Hidden posts
items.what_it_does: Hidden posts reveal extra story layers and can act like breadcrumbs for character routes.
items.player_tip: Use hidden posts to understand what a customer is really looking for before serving later drinks.

items.name: Best-ending clues
items.what_it_does: Some posts contain clues that can influence how storylines unfold and support best-ending progress.
items.player_tip: Review Tomodachill before late-game days and after major character conversations.

items.name: Seattle regulars
items.what_it_does: Special Cameo is tied to finding Tomodachill posts of Seattle regulars.
items.player_tip: Use the hashtag tab to look for posts connected to familiar faces from earlier Coffee Talk stories.

items.name: Social Media Fever
items.what_it_does: This achievement unlocks when all characters' social media profiles are unlocked.
items.player_tip: Check Tomodachill across the whole story instead of saving it only for the final day.

faq.title: Coffee Talk Tokyo FAQ
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for common Coffee Talk Tokyo questions

questions.question: Is Coffee Talk Tokyo a singleplayer game?
questions.answer: Yes. Coffee Talk Tokyo is designed as a singleplayer narrative experience focused on dialogue and drink choices.

questions.question: How do Coffee Talk Tokyo endings change?
questions.answer: Endings are shaped by dialogue choices, correct drinks, and late-story route conditions tied to character outcomes.

questions.question: Does Coffee Talk Tokyo use Tomodachill clues?
questions.answer: Yes. Tomodachill posts and hashtags provide extra context that can help with route decisions and ending setup.

questions.question: Where can I play Coffee Talk Tokyo?
questions.answer: Coffee Talk Tokyo is available on Steam, PlayStation 5, Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Switch 2, and Xbox storefronts.

questions.question: How many achievements are in Coffee Talk Tokyo?
questions.answer: Coffee Talk Tokyo has 44 achievements, covering story progress, drinks, routes, and full completion goals.
